#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT0_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_swt0_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT0, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT0 expires. This will chain the current CDT with SWT0., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT0 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT0 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT1_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT1_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_swt1_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT1, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT1 expires. This will chain the current CDT with SWT1., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT1 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT1 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT2_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT2_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_swt2_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT2, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT2 expires. This will chain the current CDT with SWT2., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT2 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT2 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT3_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_START_EMASK_BLE_SWT3_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_swt3_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT3, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT3 expires. This will chain the current CDT with SWT3., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_CDT_MATCHING_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_CDT_MATCHING_MODE (   x)

cdt_matching_mode - CDT Matching Mode enable, indicates CDT value is not a countdown value; but an absolute value to be matched with the timestamp_qus_cnt_4m counter. Added for KFOURWONE-2451, 0x0: CDT is configured in original Countdown Mode, 0x1: CDT is configured in Matching Mode

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS (   x)

expiry_emask_cs - Event Mask: CS event for KW PHY, Expiration Event Mask for triggering a CS transaction to KW PHY., 0x0: no event, 0x1: CS event triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_CDT_ADJ_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_1_CDT_ADJ_EN (   x)

cdt_adj_en - CDT timing adjustment enable, CDT timing adjustment enable for BLE 2M and BLE LR Tx/Rx for CI = 2;8., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_1_COUNTDOWN_VALUE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_1_COUNTDOWN_VALUE (   x)

countdown_value - Count Down Value, Number of quarter microseconds to count down from. Range is 1 to 8152 qus.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_INTR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_INTR (   x)

expiry_emask_intr - Event Mask: Interrupt, Expiration Event Mask for Interrupt, 0x0: no event, 0x1: interrupt triggered at expiry of timer 0b0..no event 0b1..interrupt triggered at expiry of timer

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_TX

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_TX (   x)

expiry_emask_tx - Event Mask: Tx, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Tx event triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Tx event triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_RX

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_RX (   x)

expiry_emask_rx - Event Mask: Rx, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Rx event triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Rx event triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_ABORT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_EXPIRY_EMASK_ABORT (   x)

expiry_emask_abort - Event Mask: Abort, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Abort from software., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Abort triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Abort triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_TXSTART

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_TXSTART (   x)

start_emask_ble_txstart - Event Mask: BLE Tx start, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the beginning of a BLE Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Tx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Tx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_RXSTART

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_RXSTART (   x)

start_emask_ble_rxstart - Event Mask: BLE Rx start, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the beginning of a BLE Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_TXEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_TXEND (   x)

start_emask_ble_txend - Event Mask: BLE Tx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BLE Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_RXEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_RXEND (   x)

start_emask_ble_rxend - Event Mask: BLE Rx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BLE Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Rx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Rx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BTC_PKTEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BTC_PKTEND (   x)

start_emask_btc_pktend - Event Mask: BTC PKT end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BTC Tx or Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx, or Rx) transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx or Rx) transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRHIT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRHIT (   x)

start_emask_ble_corrhit - Event Mask: BLE CORR hit, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BLE correlation hit., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation hit 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation hit

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_ASSERT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_ASSERT (   x)

start_emask_bca_req_assert - Event Mask: BCA request assertion, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BCA request assertion., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req assertion 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req assertion

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_DEASSERT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_DEASSERT (   x)

start_emask_bca_req_deassert - Event Mask: BCA request deassertion, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BCA_req de-assertion., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req de-assertion 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req de-assertion

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRMISS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRMISS (   x)

start_emask_ble_corrmiss - Event Mask: BLE CORR miss, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BLE correlation timeout interrupt., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation miss 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation miss

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_SW_TRIGGER

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_SW_TRIGGER (   x)

start_emask_sw_trigger - Event Mask: Software trigger, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when the firmware sets this bit. This bit is self clearing., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT (   x)

start_emask_hw_abort - Event Mask: Hardware abort, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when hardware abort occurs. The abort may be for a transmit packet or a receive packet., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort, happens 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort happens

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_CDT_VALUE_RESET

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_2_CDT_VALUE_RESET (   x)

cdt_value_reset - CDT value reset, This bit resets the CDT counter to 0. It will not trigger any expiry conditions (if programmed). This bit is self clearing, 0x0: no event, 0x1: Timer is reset to 0 immediately 0b0..no event 0b1..Timer is reset to 0 immediately

This bit is self clearing., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT (   x)

start_emask_hw_abort - Event Mask: Hardware abort, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when hardware abort occurs. The abort may be for a transmit packet or a receive packet., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort, happens 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort happens

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_VALUE_RESET

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_VALUE_RESET (   x)

cdt_value_reset - CDT value reset, This bit resets the CDT counter to 0. It will not trigger any expiry conditions (if programmed). This bit is self clearing, 0x0: no event, 0x1: Timer is reset to 0 immediately 0b0..no event 0b1..Timer is reset to 0 immediately

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT0_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT0_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_cdt0_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT0, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT0 expires. This will chain the current CDT with CDT0., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when CDT0 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when CDT0 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT1_EXPIRY

start_emask_ble_cdt1_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT1, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT1 expires. start_emask_ble_swt2_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT2, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT2 expires. start_emask_ble_swt3_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with SWT3, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when SWT3 expires. This will chain the current CDT with SWT3., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_MATCHING_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_MATCHING_MODE (   x)

cdt_matching_mode - CDT Matching Mode enable, indicates CDT value is not a countdown value; but an absolute value to be matched with the timestamp_qus_cnt_4m counter. Added for KFOURWONE-2451, 0x0: CDT is configured in original Countdown Mode, 0x1: CDT is configured in Matching Mode

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS (   x)

expiry_emask_cs - Event Mask: CS event for KW PHY, Expiration Event Mask for triggering a CS transaction to KW PHY., 0x0: no event, 0x1: CS event triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_ADJ_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_3_CDT_ADJ_EN (   x)

cdt_adj_en - CDT timing adjustment enable, CDT timing adjustment enable for BLE 2M and BLE LR Tx/Rx for CI = 2;8., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_3_COUNTDOWN_VALUE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_3_COUNTDOWN_VALUE (   x)

countdown_value - Count Down Value, Number of quarter microseconds to count down from. Range is 1 to 8152 qus.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_EXPIRY_EMASK_INTR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_EXPIRY_EMASK_INTR (   x)

expiry_emask_intr - Event Mask: Interrupt, Expiration Event Mask for Interrupt, 0x0: no event, 0x1: interrupt triggered at expiry of timer 0b0..no event 0b1..interrupt triggered at expiry of timer

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_EXPIRY_EMASK_TX

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_EXPIRY_EMASK_TX (   x)

expiry_emask_tx - Event Mask: Tx, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Tx event triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Tx event triggered at timer expiry

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_EXPIRY_EMASK_RX (   x)

expiry_emask_rx - Event Mask: Rx, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Rx event triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Rx event triggered at timer expiry

expiry_emask_abort - Event Mask: Abort, Expiration Event Mask for triggering an Abort from software., 0x0: no event, 0x1: Abort triggered at timer expiry 0b0..no event 0b1..Abort triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_START_EMASK_BLE_TXSTART

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_START_EMASK_BLE_TXSTART (   x)

start_emask_ble_txstart - Event Mask: BLE Tx start, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the beginning of a BLE Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Tx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Tx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_START_EMASK_BLE_RXSTART

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_4_START_EMASK_BLE_RXSTART (   x)

start_emask_ble_rxstart - Event Mask: BLE Rx start, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the beginning of a BLE Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx transaction

start_emask_ble_txend - Event Mask: BLE Tx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BLE Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx transaction

start_emask_ble_rxend - Event Mask: BLE Rx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. start_emask_btc_pktend - Event Mask: BTC PKT end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BTC Tx or Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx, or Rx) transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx or Rx) transaction

start_emask_ble_corrhit - Event Mask: BLE CORR hit, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. #define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_RXSTART (   x)

start_emask_ble_rxstart - Event Mask: BLE Rx start, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the beginning of a BLE Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at start of next BLE Rx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_TXEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_TXEND (   x)

start_emask_ble_txend - Event Mask: BLE Tx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BLE Tx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Tx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_RXEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_RXEND (   x)

start_emask_ble_rxend - Event Mask: BLE Rx end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BLE Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Rx, transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BLE Rx transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BTC_PKTEND

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BTC_PKTEND (   x)

start_emask_btc_pktend - Event Mask: BTC PKT end, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at the end of a BTC Tx or Rx transaction., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx, or Rx) transaction 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at end of next BTC packet (Tx or Rx) transaction

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRHIT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRHIT (   x)

start_emask_ble_corrhit - Event Mask: BLE CORR hit, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BLE correlation hit., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation hit 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation hit

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_ASSERT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_ASSERT (   x)

start_emask_bca_req_assert - Event Mask: BCA request assertion, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BCA request assertion., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req assertion 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req assertion

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_DEASSERT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BCA_REQ_DEASSERT (   x)

start_emask_bca_req_deassert - Event Mask: BCA request deassertion, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BCA_req de-assertion., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req de-assertion 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BCA_req de-assertion

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRMISS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CORRMISS (   x)

start_emask_ble_corrmiss - Event Mask: BLE CORR miss, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting at BLE correlation timeout interrupt., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation miss 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from programmed value at next BLE correlation miss

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_SW_TRIGGER

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_SW_TRIGGER (   x)

start_emask_sw_trigger - Event Mask: Software trigger, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when the firmware sets this bit. This bit is self clearing., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value as soon as this bit is set

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_HW_ABORT (   x)

start_emask_hw_abort - Event Mask: Hardware abort, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when hardware abort occurs. The abort may be for a transmit packet or a receive packet., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort, happens 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down from the programmed value bit when hardware abort happens

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_VALUE_RESET

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_VALUE_RESET (   x)

cdt_value_reset - CDT value reset, This bit resets the CDT counter to 0. It will not trigger any expiry conditions (if programmed). This bit is self clearing, 0x0: no event, 0x1: Timer is reset to 0 immediately 0b0..no event 0b1..Timer is reset to 0 immediately

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT0_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT0_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_cdt0_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT0, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT0 expires. This will chain the current CDT with CDT0., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when CDT0 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when CDT0 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT1_EXPIRY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT1_EXPIRY (   x)

start_emask_ble_cdt1_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT1, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT1 expires. This will chain the current CDT with CDT1., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when CDT1 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when CDT1 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_START_EMASK_BLE_CDT2_EXPIRY

start_emask_ble_cdt2_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT2, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT2 expires. This will chain the current CDT with CDT2., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when CDT2 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when CDT2 expires

start_emask_ble_cdt3_expiry - Event Mask: Chain with CDT3, Start Event Mask for triggering the CDT counter. Start counting when CDT3 expires. This will chain the current CDT with CDT3., 0x0: No event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when CDT3 expires 0b0..No event 0b1..timer starts counting down when CDT3 expires

This will chain the current CDT with SWT3., 0x0: no event, 0x1: timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires 0b0..no event 0b1..timer starts counting down when SWT3 expires

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_MATCHING_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_MATCHING_MODE (   x)

cdt_matching_mode - CDT Matching Mode enable, indicates CDT value is not a countdown value; but an absolute value to be matched with the timestamp_qus_cnt_4m counter. Added for KFOURWONE-2451, 0x0: CDT is configured in original Countdown Mode, 0x1: CDT is configured in Matching Mode

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_EXPIRY_EMASK_CS (   x)

expiry_emask_cs - Event Mask: CS event for KW PHY, Expiration Event Mask for triggering a CS transaction to KW PHY., 0x0: no event, 0x1: CS event triggered at timer expiry

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_ADJ_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CTL_5_CDT_ADJ_EN (   x)

cdt_adj_en - CDT timing adjustment enable, CDT timing adjustment enable for BLE 2M and BLE LR Tx/Rx for CI = 2;8., 0x0: disable, 0x1: enable 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_5_COUNTDOWN_VALUE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_VALUE_5_COUNTDOWN_VALUE (   x)

countdown_value - Count Down Value, Number of quarter microseconds to count down from. Range is 1 to 8152 qus.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_2M_DATA_ADJ_RX_ADJ_VALUE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_2M_DATA_ADJ_RX_ADJ_VALUE (   x)

rx_adj_value - Rx Count Down Value in QUS, +/- offset. Resolution: 0/25 us

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_2M_DATA_ADJ_RX_ADJ_SIGN

rx_adj_sign - Rx Operation Sign, 0x0: add, 0x1: substrate 0b0..add 0b1..substrate

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_CI_RX_DATA_ADJ_4_RX_CI_ADJ_EMASK (   x)

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_STS_cdt_expiry_sts

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TMR_CDT_STS_cdt_expiry_sts (   x)

cdt_expiry_sts - CDT Expiry Status in CDT Matching mode, This bit is updated at each CDT expiry; when CDT is programmed in cdt_matching_mode. For the purpose of this register; past value on CDT refers to any value less than the half-slot+qus timestamp at which CDT was programmed. Once set it remains set until this CDT expires again in the matching mode, 0x0: CDT matching was successful, 0x1: CDT matching value was in the past at the time of programming

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_1M

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_1M (   x)

tx_ant_switch_delay_1m - Antenna Switch Delay from end of Tx Crc, Timer delayed from end of CRC of the current Tx packet to start driving requestAntenna to signal BCA which antenna is used for the Tx extension field., - Resolution: 0.25 usec.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_2M

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_2M (   x)

tx_ant_switch_delay_2m - Antenna Switch Delay from end of Tx Crc, Timer delayed from end of CRC of the current Tx packet to start driving requestAntenna to signal BCA which antenna is used for the Tx extension field. This delay is for BLE Tx 2 Mbps., - Resolution: 0.25 usec.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_MAP_LENGTH

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_MAP_LENGTH (   x)

tx_ant_map_length - Antenna Switch Map Length for Tx DFE, - Valid length of Tx antenna map pattern (max: 40). After executing the antenna switching for this length; BLE will repeat the pattern from the beginning. The map is used when BLE is transmitting extension packets.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_RATE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_RATE (   x)

tx_ant_switch_rate - Antenna Switch Rate, Define the antenna switching rate for extension packet., 0x0: switch antenna every 4 usec, 0x1: switch antenna every 2 usec, 0x2: switch antenna every 1 usec (RFU), 0x3: not valid 0b00..switch antenna every 4 usec 0b01..switch antenna every 2 usec 0b10..switch antenna every 1 usec (RFU) 0b11..not valid

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_REF_ANT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_REF_ANT (   x)

ref_ant - BCA reference antenna, The value of reference antenna held by the BCA.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_CNTRL_TX_ANT_SWITCH_EN (   x)

tx_ant_switch_en - Antenna Switch Enable for Tx DFE, Switch antenna for extension when transmitting location packet., 0x0: disable antenna switch for Tx DFE, 0x1: enable antenna switch for Tx DFE 0b0..disable antenna switch for Tx DFE 0b1..enable antenna switch for Tx DFE

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0_TX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_0

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0_TX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_0 (   x)

tx_ant_switch_index_0 - Antenna Switch Map, Antenna switch map is programmed in a group of 10; 32-bit registers; which represents 40 different values; each value being 5-bit wide for 32 antennas. Switching pattern starts from BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0; and follows the index order of tx_ant_switch_index_0.1.2.to 3; while BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0 finishes; continues with the other 4 indexes in BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_1.2.to 9. The pattern length use is determined by tx_ant_map_length.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0_TX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_1

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0_TX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_1 (   x)

tx_ant_switch_index_1 - Antenna Switch Map, Antenna switch map is programmed in a group of 10; 32-bit registers; which represents 40 different values; each value being 5-bit wide for 32 antennas. Switching pattern starts from BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0; and follows the index order of tx_ant_switch_index_0.1.2.to 3; while BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0 finishes; continues with the other 4 indexes in BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_1.2.to 9. The pattern length use is determined by tx_ant_map_length.

tx_ant_switch_index_3 - Antenna Switch Map, Antenna switch map is programmed in a group of 10; 32-bit registers; which represents 40 different values; each value being 5-bit wide for 32 antennas. Switching pattern starts from BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0; and follows the index order of tx_ant_switch_index_0.1.2.to 3; while BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0 finishes; continues with the other 4 indexes in BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_1.2.to 9. The pattern length use is determined by tx_ant_map_length.

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_9_TX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_3 (   x)

tx_ant_switch_index_3 - Antenna Switch Map, Antenna switch map is programmed in a group of 10; 32-bit registers; which represents 40 different values; each value being 5-bit wide for 32 antennas. Switching pattern starts from BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0; and follows the index order of tx_ant_switch_index_0.1.2.to 3; while BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_0 finishes; continues with the other 4 indexes in BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_TX_MAP_1.2.to 9. ◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_1M

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_1M (   x)

rx_ant_switch_delay_1m - Antenna Switch Delay from start of Rx Crc, Timer delayed from start of CRC of the current Rx packet to start driving requestAntenna to signal BCA which antenna is used for the expected Rx extension field., - Resolution: 0.25 usec.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_2M

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_DELAY_2M (   x)

rx_ant_switch_delay_2m - Antenna Switch Delay from start of Rx Crc, Timer delayed from start of CRC of the current Rx packet to start driving requestAntenna to signal BCA which antenna is used for the expected Rx extension field. This delay is for BLE Rx 2 Mbps., - Resolution: 0.25 usec.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_MAP_LENGTH

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_MAP_LENGTH (   x)

rx_ant_map_length - Antenna Switch Map Length for Rx DFE, - Valid length of Rx antenna map pattern (max: 40). After executing the antenna switching for this length; BLE will repeat the pattern from the beginning. The map is used when BLE is receiving extension packets.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_RATE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_RATE (   x)

rx_ant_switch_rate - Antenna Switch Rate, Define the antenna switching rate for extension packet., 0x0: switch antenna every 4 usec, 0x1: switch antenna every 2 usec, 0x2: switch antenna every 1 usec (RFU), 0x3: not valid 0b00..switch antenna every 4 usec 0b01..switch antenna every 2 usec 0b10..switch antenna every 1 usec (RFU) 0b11..not valid

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_REF_ANT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_REF_ANT (   x)

ref_ant - BCA reference antenna, The value of reference antenna held by BCA.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_CNTRL_RX_ANT_SWITCH_EN (   x)

rx_ant_switch_en - Antenna Switch Enable for Rx DFE, Switch antenna for extension when receiving location packet., 0x0: disable antenna switch for Rx DFE, 0x1: enable antenna switch for Rx DFE 0b0..disable antenna switch for Rx DFE 0b1..enable antenna switch for Rx DFE

Switching pattern starts from BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_MAP_0; and follows the index order of rx_ant_switch_index_0.1.2.to 3; while BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_MAP_0 finishes; continues with the other 4 indexes in BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_MAP_1.2.to 9. The pattern length use is determined by rx_ant_map_length.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_ANT_SWITCH_RX_MAP_5_RX_ANT_SWITCH_INDEX_2

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_ISO_TMR_EN (   x)

iso_tmr_en - Enable Periodic SW Timer's ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TIMER_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TIMER_MODE (   x)

timer_mode - Configure Peridodic SW Timer Timer Mode 0b0..configure to BIS timer mode 0b1..configure to CIS timer mode

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_SEQ_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_SEQ_MODE (   x)

seq_mode - Configure Multiple BISes/CISes To Sequential Or Interleaved Arrangement 0b0..configure to interleaved arrangement 0b1..configure to sequential arrangement

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_TX_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_TX_EVT (   x)

trig_tx_evt - Trigger a Packet Tx Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_RX_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_RX_EVT (   x)

trig_rx_evt - Trigger a Packet Rx Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_INTR_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_TRIG_INTR_EVT (   x)

trig_intr_evt - Trigger Interrupt Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. If timer_mode is BIS timer mode, bis_anchr_intr will trigger, otherwise, cis_anchr_intr will.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_SYNC_DELAY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_SYNC_DELAY_INTR_EN (   x)

sync_delay_intr_en - Trigger Interrupt Event Upon Sync_Dly_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. If timer_mode is BIS timer mode, big_sync_delay_intr will trigger, otherwise, cig_sync_delay_intr will.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_EARLY_INTR_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_EARLY_INTR_TIME (   x)

early_intr_time - Early Interrupt Assertion Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_EARLY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_0_EARLY_INTR_EN (   x)

early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For bis_anchr_intr or cis_anchr_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the bis_anchr_intr or cis_anchr_intr will be asserted ealier than anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_0_CURR_BIS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_0_CURR_BIS (   x)

curr_bis - Current Active BIS/CIS Number

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_0_BIS_ANCHR_INTR

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_0_CIS_ANCHR_INTR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_0_CIS_ANCHR_INTR (   x)

cis_anchr_intr - CIS Anchor Interrupt

curr_subevt - Current Active Subevent Number

cig_sync_delay_intr - CIG_Sync_Delay Interrupt

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_FRST_ANCHR_0_FRST_ANCHR_NAT_CLK_VAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_FRST_ANCHR_0_FRST_ANCHR_NAT_CLK_VAL (   x)

frst_anchr_nat_clk_val - First Anchor Native Clock Value

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_0_FRST_ANCHR_START_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_0_FRST_ANCHR_START_TIME (   x)

frst_anchr_start_time - First Anchor Start Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_0_CURR_ANCHR_START_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_0_CURR_ANCHR_START_TIME (   x)

curr_anchr_start_time - Current Anchor Start Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ANCHR_CORR_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ANCHR_CORR_TIME (   x)

anchr_corr_time - Anchor Point To Correlation Hit Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ADJ_AST_OFST

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ADJ_AST_OFST (   x)

adj_ast_ofst - Adjusted Anchor Start Time Offset

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ADJ_AST_DIR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_0_ADJ_AST_DIR (   x)

adj_ast_dir - Adjusted Anchor Start Time Direction 0b0..Substract adj_ast_ofst from ISO_Intvl_Tmr value counting toward next BIS/CIS event, effectively moving next BIS/CIS anchor point to a earlier time. This is used when the averaged anchr_corr_time value is gradually decreasing, indicating the slave device's local clock is drifting slower. 0b1..Add adj_ast_ofst to ISO_Intvl_Tmr value counting toward next BIS/CIS event, effectively delaying next BIS/CIS anchor point to a later time. This is used when the averaged anchr_corr_time value is gradually increasing, indicating the slave device's local clock is drifting faster.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_ISO_INTERVAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_ISO_INTERVAL (   x)

iso_interval - Configure ISO Interval Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_CURR_ISO_INTVL_TMR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_CURR_ISO_INTVL_TMR (   x)

curr_iso_intvl_tmr - Current ISO_Intvl_Tmr Timer Value

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_BIT15_0_READ_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_BIT15_0_READ_MODE (   x)

bit15_0_read_mode - Bit[15:0] iso_interval field read back value 0b0..iso_interval field will read back ISO_Interval time of BIS/CIS event being programmed. Unit is 1.25ms. 0b1..iso_interval field will read back curr_iso_intvl_tmr_ofst[10:0] at the time of snapshot_iso_intvl_tmr command being programmed. Unit is 0.25 us.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_SNAPSHOT_ISO_INTVL_TMR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_0_SNAPSHOT_ISO_INTVL_TMR (   x)

snapshot_iso_intvl_tmr - Take a snapshot of ISO_Intvl_Tmr and offset counter value

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SPC_0_BIS_SPACING

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SPC_0_BIS_SPACING (   x)

bis_spacing - Configure BIS Spacing Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SPC_0_NUM_BIS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SPC_0_NUM_BIS (   x)

num_bis - Configure Number of BISes/CISes in BIG/CIG

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SEL_STRM_0_SEL_STREAM

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SEL_STRM_0_SEL_STREAM (   x)

sel_stream - Select Active Streams

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SYNC_DLY_0_SYNC_DELAY (   x)

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_SUB_TMR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_SUB_TMR_EN (   x)

sub_tmr_en - Enable Periodic SW Timer's Sub_Intvl_Tmr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_TX_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_TX_SUBEVT (   x)

trig_tx_subevt - Trigger a Packet Tx Event Upon Sub_Intvl_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_RX_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_RX_SUBEVT (   x)

trig_rx_subevt - Trigger a Packet Rx Event Upon Sub_Intvl_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_INTR_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_TRIG_INTR_SUBEVT (   x)

trig_intr_subevt - Trigger Interrupt Event Upon Sub_Intvl_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. Either sub_event_intr or ctrl_subevt_intr (if has_ctrl_subevt = 1) will trigger.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN (   x)

subevt_early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For sub_event_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the sub_event_intr will be asserted ealier than subevent anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_CTRL_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_CTRL_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN (   x)

ctrl_subevt_early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For ctrl_subevt_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the ctrl_subevt_intr will be asserted ealier than control subevent anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_HAS_CTRL_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_0_HAS_CTRL_SUBEVT (   x)

has_ctrl_subevt - Configure To Have Control Subevent In BIS Event 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. Enalbing time must meet in order to have control subevent triggered in current BIS event. Only applicable in timer_mode = 0 (BIS timer mode).

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_0_SUB_INTERVAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_0_SUB_INTERVAL (   x)

sub_interval - Configure Subevent Interval Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_0_NSE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_0_NSE (   x)

nse - Configure Number of Subevents Per BIS/CIS Event

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_DIS_0_DIS_REMAIN_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_DIS_0_DIS_REMAIN_SUBEVT (   x)

dis_remain_subevt - Disable Remaining Subevents

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_ISO_TMR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_ISO_TMR_EN (   x)

iso_tmr_en - Enable Periodic SW Timer's ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TIMER_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TIMER_MODE (   x)

timer_mode - Configure Peridodic SW Timer Timer Mode 0b0..configure to BIS timer mode 0b1..configure to CIS timer mode

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_SEQ_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_SEQ_MODE (   x)

seq_mode - Configure Multiple BISes/CISes To Sequential Or Interleaved Arrangement 0b0..configure to interleaved arrangement 0b1..configure to sequential arrangement

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_TX_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_TX_EVT (   x)

trig_tx_evt - Trigger a Packet Tx Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_RX_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_RX_EVT (   x)

trig_rx_evt - Trigger a Packet Rx Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_INTR_EVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_TRIG_INTR_EVT (   x)

trig_intr_evt - Trigger Interrupt Event Upon ISO_Intvl_Tmr/BIS_Spc_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. If timer_mode is BIS timer mode, bis_anchr_intr will trigger, otherwise, cis_anchr_intr will.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_SYNC_DELAY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_SYNC_DELAY_INTR_EN (   x)

sync_delay_intr_en - Trigger Interrupt Event Upon Sync_Dly_Tmr Timer Expiry 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. If timer_mode is BIS timer mode, big_sync_delay_intr will trigger, otherwise, cig_sync_delay_intr will.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_EARLY_INTR_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_EARLY_INTR_TIME (   x)

early_intr_time - Early Interrupt Assertion Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_EARLY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_CTRL_1_EARLY_INTR_EN (   x)

early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For bis_anchr_intr or cis_anchr_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the bis_anchr_intr or cis_anchr_intr will be asserted ealier than anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CURR_BIS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CURR_BIS (   x)

curr_bis - Current Active BIS/CIS Number

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CIS_ANCHR_INTR

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CURR_SUBEVT

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CIG_SYNC_DELAY_INTR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_STS_1_CIG_SYNC_DELAY_INTR (   x)

cig_sync_delay_intr - CIG_Sync_Delay Interrupt

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_FRST_ANCHR_1_FRST_ANCHR_NAT_CLK_VAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_FRST_ANCHR_1_FRST_ANCHR_NAT_CLK_VAL (   x)

frst_anchr_nat_clk_val - First Anchor Native Clock Value

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_1_FRST_ANCHR_START_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_1_FRST_ANCHR_START_TIME (   x)

frst_anchr_start_time - First Anchor Start Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_1_CURR_ANCHR_START_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_OFST_1_CURR_ANCHR_START_TIME (   x)

curr_anchr_start_time - Current Anchor Start Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ANCHR_CORR_TIME

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ANCHR_CORR_TIME (   x)

anchr_corr_time - Anchor Point To Correlation Hit Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ADJ_AST_OFST

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ADJ_AST_OFST (   x)

adj_ast_ofst - Adjusted Anchor Start Time Offset

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ADJ_AST_DIR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_ANCHR_ADJ_1_ADJ_AST_DIR (   x)

adj_ast_dir - Adjusted Anchor Start Time Direction 0b0..Substract adj_ast_ofst from ISO_Intvl_Tmr value counting toward next BIS/CIS event, effectively moving next BIS/CIS anchor point to a earlier time. This is used when the averaged anchr_corr_time value is gradually decreasing, indicating the slave device's local clock is drifting slower. 0b1..Add adj_ast_ofst to ISO_Intvl_Tmr value counting toward next BIS/CIS event, effectively delaying next BIS/CIS anchor point to a later time. This is used when the averaged anchr_corr_time value is gradually increasing, indicating the slave device's local clock is drifting faster.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_ISO_INTERVAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_ISO_INTERVAL (   x)

iso_interval - Configure ISO Interval Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_CURR_ISO_INTVL_TMR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_CURR_ISO_INTVL_TMR (   x)

curr_iso_intvl_tmr - Current ISO_Intvl_Tmr Timer Value

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_BIT15_0_READ_MODE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_BIT15_0_READ_MODE (   x)

bit15_0_read_mode - Bit[15:0] iso_interval field read back value 0b0..iso_interval field will read back ISO_Interval time of BIS/CIS event being programmed. Unit is 1.25ms. 0b1..iso_interval field will read back curr_iso_intvl_tmr_ofst[10:0] at the time of snapshot_iso_intvl_tmr command being programmed. Unit is 0.25 us.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_SNAPSHOT_ISO_INTVL_TMR

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_INTVL_1_SNAPSHOT_ISO_INTVL_TMR (   x)

snapshot_iso_intvl_tmr - Take a snapshot of ISO_Intvl_Tmr and offset counter value

ctrl_subevt_early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For ctrl_subevt_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the ctrl_subevt_intr will be asserted ealier than control subevent anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

subevt_early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For sub_event_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the sub_event_intr will be asserted ealier than subevent anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_2_CTRL_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_2_CTRL_SUBEVT_EARLY_INTR_EN (   x)

ctrl_subevt_early_intr_en - Enable Early Interrupt Assertion For ctrl_subevt_intr 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. When enabled, the ctrl_subevt_intr will be asserted ealier than control subevent anchor point by the specified early_intr_time time.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_2_HAS_CTRL_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_CTRL_2_HAS_CTRL_SUBEVT (   x)

has_ctrl_subevt - Configure To Have Control Subevent In BIS Event 0b0..disable 0b1..enable. Enalbing time must meet in order to have control subevent triggered in current BIS event. Only applicable in timer_mode = 0 (BIS timer mode).

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_2_SUB_INTERVAL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_2_SUB_INTERVAL (   x)

sub_interval - Configure Subevent Interval Time

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_2_NSE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_INTVL_2_NSE (   x)

nse - Configure Number of Subevents Per BIS/CIS Event

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_DIS_2_DIS_REMAIN_SUBEVT

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_PST_SE_DIS_2_DIS_REMAIN_SUBEVT (   x)

dis_remain_subevt - Disable Remaining Subevents

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RD_ST

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RD_ST (   x)

rd_st - rd_st value, Current value of ble_rd state machine rd_st.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RESET_RD_ST

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RESET_RD_ST (   x)

reset_rd_st - reset rd_st and mst_arb_st, To reset rd_st state machine back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit. If ble_ahb_arb FSM mst_arb_st is servicing ble_rd; it will be reset together.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_TD_ST

td_st - td_st value, Current value of ble_td state machine td_st.

reset_td_st - reset td_st and mst_arb_st, To reset td_st state machine back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit. If ble_ahb_arb FSM mst_arb_st is servicing ble_td; it will be reset together.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHBW_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RL_ST

reset_rl_st - reset rl_st and mst_arb_st, To reset rl_st state machine back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit. If ble_ahb_arb FSM mst_arb_st is servicing ble_reg_ldr; it will be reset together.

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_TX_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RESET_TX_MEM_CTL (   x)

reset_tx_mem_ctl - reset the 2 state machines in ble_tx_mem_ctl, To reset both tx_rd_st and tx_mem_rd_st state machine back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit.

rx_pld_blk_rdy_st - rx_pld_blk_rdy_st value, Current value of ble_ccm_ctl state machine rx_pld_blk_rdy_st.

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_CCM_FSM_MON_CNTRL_RESET_CCM_CTL (   x)

reset_ccm_ctl - reset the 2 state machines in ble_ccm_ctl, To reset both rx_pld_blk_rdy_st and ccm_ctl_st state machines back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_CCM_FSM_MON_CNTRL_ARB_ST

reset_btu_ccm_aes - reset the 2 state machines in ble_ccm_aes, To reset both arb_st and ccm_st state machines back to Idle state; firmware first writes 1 and then writes 0 to this bit.

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_CNTRL_BLE_AHB_MON_SEL (   x)

ble_ahb_mon_sel - BLE AHB Master Debug Signals Selection, BLE AHB Master Debug Signals Selection, Select BLE AHB Data Bus, 0x0: hRdData[7:0], 0x1: hRdData[15:8], 0x2: hRdData[23:16], 0x3: hRdData[31:24], 0x4: hWrData[7:0], 0x5: hWrData[15:8], 0x6: hWrData[23:16], 0x7: hWrData[31:24]

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_BANK_SEL_TESTBUS_BANK_SELECT_3 (   x)

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_BANK_SEL_TESTBUS_BANK_SELECT_4

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_BANK_SEL_TESTBUS_BANK_SELECT_4 (   x)

testbus_bank_select_4 - BLE debug test bus selection [15:12]

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_IP_REVISION_BTU_IP_REV [1/2]

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_IP_REVISION_BTU_IP_REV (   x)

BTU_IP_Rev - BLE IP Revision

btu_ip_rev - BLE IP Revision, Bits[15:8] = major revision number (incremented at full tapeout), Bits[7:0] = minor revision number (incremented at post tapeout ECO), 88W8787 (Robin) = 0x0800 88W8790 (uBB) = 0x0800 88W8797 (Starling) = 0x900 88W8766 (Geagle) = 0x0900 88W8897 (Kfisher) = 0x1100 88W8777 (Robin2) = 0x1200 88W8887 (CardiAC) = 0x1300 8830 (Sunbird/BTHM) = 0x1400 88W8977 (Robin3/BTU2) = 0x1600 88W8997 (Kfisher2/BTU2) = 0x1700 88W8987 (Cardi2/BTU2) = 0x1700 88W9064 (SCBT/BTU2) = 0x2100 88W9098 (Skyhawk/BTU2) = 0x2200 88W9000 (Kestrel/BTU2) = 0x2400 88W8978 (Robin3P/BTU2) = 0x2500 Firecrest/BTU2 = 0x2700 K4W1 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x2800 Redfinch/BLE_only = 0x2600 Owl/BTU2 = 0x2900 Tasman/BLE_only = 0x3000 Kamet/BTU2 = 0x3100 Blackbird/BTU2 = 0x3200 KW47 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3400 Hood/BTU2 = 0x3500 Fuji (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3600 MW30 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3700

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_IP_REVISION_BTU_IP_REV [2/2]

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_IP_REVISION_BTU_IP_REV (   x)

BTU_IP_Rev - BLE IP Revision

btu_ip_rev - BLE IP Revision, Bits[15:8] = major revision number (incremented at full tapeout), Bits[7:0] = minor revision number (incremented at post tapeout ECO), 88W8787 (Robin) = 0x0800 88W8790 (uBB) = 0x0800 88W8797 (Starling) = 0x900 88W8766 (Geagle) = 0x0900 88W8897 (Kfisher) = 0x1100 88W8777 (Robin2) = 0x1200 88W8887 (CardiAC) = 0x1300 8830 (Sunbird/BTHM) = 0x1400 88W8977 (Robin3/BTU2) = 0x1600 88W8997 (Kfisher2/BTU2) = 0x1700 88W8987 (Cardi2/BTU2) = 0x1700 88W9064 (SCBT/BTU2) = 0x2100 88W9098 (Skyhawk/BTU2) = 0x2200 88W9000 (Kestrel/BTU2) = 0x2400 88W8978 (Robin3P/BTU2) = 0x2500 Firecrest/BTU2 = 0x2700 K4W1 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x2800 Redfinch/BLE_only = 0x2600 Owl/BTU2 = 0x2900 Tasman/BLE_only = 0x3000 Kamet/BTU2 = 0x3100 Blackbird/BTU2 = 0x3200 KW47 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3400 Hood/BTU2 = 0x3500 Fuji (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3600 MW30 (NBU/BTU2) = 0x3700

ble_tx_aes_mrgn_min - ble_tx_aes_mrgn_min, Minimum AES operating margin in quarter us during TX

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHB_LAT_BLE_MST_TXN_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_AHB_LAT_BLE_MST_TXN_EN (   x)

ble_mst_txn_en - ble_mst_txn_en, Enable BLE master transaction latency monitor. When disabled; the maximum latency is reset to 0

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_CPU_GATEHCLK_SEL

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_CPU_GATEHCLK_SEL (   x)

ble_cpu_gatehclk_sel - BLE CPU Gatehclk Select 0b0..gatehclk connected to GATEHCLK port from BLE CPU 0b1..gatehclk connected to SLEEPING port from BLE CPU

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BRF_PU_OPT_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BRF_PU_OPT_EN (   x)

brf_pu_opt_en - BRF powerup Optimization Enable, Enable BRF_PU optimization to save power in BRF-FRF cross connect mode of Firecrest A1

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_NAT_CLK_SLEEP_WR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_NAT_CLK_SLEEP_WR_EN (   x)

ble_nat_clk_sleep_wr_en - BLE Native Clock Sleep write enable, Enable CPU write to BLE Native Clock in sleep domain. FW needs to set this bit before writing to BLE_REG_TMR_NATIVE_CLOCK register; and must clear this bit after the write is done.

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_SW_MBOX_CLR_EN

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_SW_MBOX_CLR_EN (   x)

ble_sw_mbox_clr_en - BLE SW_Mbox_Clr_En

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_MASK_REQ_AT_SLEEP_REQ_RANGE

ble_mask_req_at_sleep_req_range - BLE Mask Request at Sleep_Req_Range

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_SWTPAST_ECO_DISABLE

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_SWTPAST_ECO_DISABLE (   x)

swtpast_eco_disable - SWT past Intr ECO disable, Disable bit for SWT past intr WIPBTU-478 fix, 0x0: WIPBTU-478 fix is enabled in Owl A1, 0x1: WIPBTU-478 fix is disabled; older logic preserved

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_MBOX_REQ_DELAY

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_BLE_MBOX_REQ_DELAY (   x)

ble_mbox_req_delay - BLE Mbox Request Delay

◆ BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_CCA_COEX_ECO_DIS

#define BLE2_REG_BLE_REG_DEBUG_RVSD_CCA_COEX_ECO_DIS (   x)

cca_coex_eco_dis - CCA Coex ECO disable, Disable bit for CCA BTC+BLE coex TXPkt/TxAbort counters WIPBTU-546, 0x0: WIPBTU-546 fix is enabled in BB A1, 0x1: WIPBTU-546 fix is disabled; older logic preserved
